queries.query: AI powered kids coding education trends 2026
keyFindings: 'Vibe coding' (AI-assisted real-time generation) maturing in 2026 — kids apps should embrace co-creation framing
AI literacy + architectural thinking now prioritized over syntax memorization; apps should teach prompting skills
Global AI education market hit $7.57B in 2025, projected $112B by 2034 — strong tailwinds for Inspiror
1:1 AI tutoring sessions with real-time feedback are the leading personalization model
Code Edu launched 'Encode' AI creative learning platform at AI Impact Summit 2026 — direct competitor signal

queries.query: Rosebud AI Bolt.new Replit Codorex competitor updates March 2026
keyFindings: Replit raised $400M Series D; Agent 4 can build and maintain entire companies autonomously — pivoting further from K-12

Bolt.new and Lovable remain dominant in vibe-coding space; primary audience is adult developers, not children
Rosebud AI published 'Alternative to Bolt.new' blog — signals it is repositioning toward creative/game-making audience
No Codorex-specific updates found; market consolidating around Replit / Bolt / Lovable for general coding

queries.query: UX gamification kids coding tools research 2026
keyFindings: Role-playing and cooperation mechanics most effective for flow experience in 8–10 age group (295-student study, PLOS ONE)
Puzzle games most effective for motivation; reasoning strategy games best for academic achievement (meta-analysis, ScienceDirect)
Gamification largest positive effect on motivation, then achievement, least on cognitive load — keep UI simple
Children react negatively to content even one grade above/below their level — age-adaptive difficulty is critical
Instant, exaggerated reward loops + scaffolded pacing are the UX gold standard for kids coding tools

queries.query: most engaging features kids coding games 2025 2026 retention what makes kids come back scratch roblox
keyFindings: Starter templates beat blank canvas for first-session retention
Expressive mascots (Duolingo owl) are top engagement driver per CHI 2025
Social sharing and community galleries are #1 return-visit driver (Scratch, Roblox)
Progressive difficulty and surprise unlocks keep kids returning
Time-to-delight is #1 predictor of retention in kids apps

queries.query: PWA offline kids apps best practices 2026
keyFindings: Service worker + cache-first for static assets, network-first for API, stale-while-revalidate for dynamic content
All PWA features must degrade gracefully offline — critical for classroom environments with spotty wifi
Web App Manifest + HTTPS required; responsive design across all input types (touch, mouse, stylus)
PWA install prompt + home-screen icon significantly boosts re-engagement for kids apps
Inspiror opportunity: cache templates and blocks offline so kids can prototype without network

queries.query: kids coding app onboarding tutorial patterns first-run experience UX 2026
keyFindings: Duolingo model: complete first meaningful interaction within 60 seconds of launch — no long tutorial screens
Only 23.9% iOS / 21.1% Android users return after Day 1 — first-run delight is existential
Kids coding apps must let children play/explore before asking them to code — 'try first, explain later'
Progressive disclosure: introduce one concept at a time; avoid feature dumps in welcome flow
Weekly engaged users are 90% more likely to retain long-term — onboarding goal is habit formation, not feature education

queries.query: dark mode implementation patterns kids apps React Tailwind CSS 2026
keyFindings: Tailwind v4 dark mode: set darkMode:'class' in config, toggle 'dark' class on <html>; CSS variables + React Context is the scalable 2026 pattern
Use prefers-color-scheme media query as the default (system preference); allow user override stored in localStorage
Prevent flash-of-unstyled-content (FOUC) with an inline <script> in index.html that reads localStorage before React hydrates
For kids apps, 'candy dark' (dark BG + vivid accent colors) outperforms simple greyscale inversion — maintain brand color palette in dark mode
tailwindcss-theme-variants plugin enables named themes (light/dark/high-contrast) as Tailwind prefixes — good for a11y compliance
61% of engineering teams deploy dark mode via feature flags (LaunchDarkly 2024 report) — low-risk rollout strategy

queries.query: Content Security Policy CSP iframe sandbox best practices 2025 2026
keyFindings: Inspiror's PreviewPanel already uses sandbox='allow-scripts' (correct — no allow-same-origin, no allow-forms) — this is the gold standard for user-code sandboxes
Helmet default CSP may block srcdoc iframes: need to verify frame-src 'none' is not set; srcdoc iframes do NOT load an external URL so frame-src policy applies differently across browsers
Add explicit Content-Security-Policy header on the Express server: frame-ancestors 'self' (blocks clickjacking), default-src 'self', script-src 'self' 'nonce-{nonce}' for any inline scripts
For srcdoc iframes running user JS, postMessage origin must be validated on parent side (Inspiror already does this in useAutoFix.ts via event.origin check)
OWASP 2026: CSP Level 3 with 'strict-dynamic' + nonces is preferred over allowlisted origins for script-src — reduces XSS risk even if attacker controls a CDN
All iframe content should load over HTTPS only; in 2026 any third-party that doesn't support HTTPS is a red flag (not applicable to Inspiror's srcdoc model)

queries.query: security advisories React Vite Express Node.js 2026 CVE
keyFindings: CVE-2025-55182 (CVSS 10.0): React Server Components RCE — affects React 19.0/19.1/19.2 with RSC; Inspiror uses React without RSC so NOT directly affected, but monitor
CVE-2025-55184 + CVE-2025-67779 (CVSS 7.5 High): DoS via crafted HTTP to Server Functions endpoint — RSC-only, same exemption applies
CVE-2026-23864: additional DoS in RSC — patched; again RSC-only
Node.js Jan 2026 security release: --permission model bypass via symlink paths; HTTP/2 HPACK crash (remote DoS); UDS bypass for privilege escalation — update to latest LTS (22.x) recommended
Node.js OpenSSL advisory Jan 2026: patch included in regular Node.js releases — handled by Node.js LTS update
CVE-2025-58752 (Vite fs bypass) was already captured in state — confirm Vite is patched to ≥5.4.x or ≥6.x
No new critical Express CVEs found as of March 2026 — existing helmet + rate-limit + input validation in Inspiror backend covers known attack surface

queries.query: AI co-creation pedagogy children + prompt scaffolding + metacognition + OECD AI literacy + Scratch Copilot + gamification
keyFindings: ACM IDC 2025: 6 design considerations for child-AI co-creation (agency, transparency, explainability, safety, equity, iteration)
Vygotsky ZPD: AI as 'more capable peer' with fading scaffolds is the gold standard for learning
ScienceDirect 2025 meta-analysis: scaffolded prompt templates significantly outperform free-text for AI literacy
MIT Scratch Copilot 2025: 50% of kids (8-12) rejected AI suggestions to preserve originality — ownership matters deeply
Frontiers 2025 Cognitive Mirror: post-generation reflection questions are the #1 metacognitive intervention
OECD 2025 AI Literacy Framework: 7 competencies for ages 8-14 (create, evaluate, collaborate, communicate, problem-solve, think critically, self-aware)
HASRL model: child sets goals, AI executes, child reflects — never generate unprompted
Taylor & Francis 2025: badge process behaviors (iterate, reflect, edit) over outcomes (finished game)
Nature Human Behaviour 2024: human-AI teams outperform solo only when human contributes creativity/judgment

queries.query: generative AI creative tools kids sound music art 2025 2026
keyFindings: Google DeepMind Lyria 3 now in Gemini app (March 2026): text-prompt → 30-second music track + cover art; directly callable via Gemini API Inspiror already uses
Soundverse AI generates kids songs from simple prompts ('a happy tune about friendship') in 10+ languages — sets UX precedent for in-app sound generation
Suno AI reached 100M users with $250M raise ($2.45B valuation) — AI music creation is now mainstream, not niche
Opportunity: Inspiror could add 'Add background music' button — child describes mood, Lyria 3 generates 30s loop for their game via Gemini API
AI art gen (DALL-E, Imagen) could power 'Generate sprite' button — child describes character, AI returns PNG for use in game canvas
Precedent gap: no kids coding tool currently bundles AI-generated audio/art assets into the creation flow — genuine differentiation opportunity

queries.query: kids coding app social features community gallery showcase 2025 2026
keyFindings: Hopscotch, Scratch, Tynker all identify community gallery + project showcase as #1 return-visit driver for ages 8-14
Coolest Projects global showcase (Jan 2026) demonstrates demand: children want external recognition, not just personal achievement
Tynker Code Jam: in-app competitions with peer judging drive engagement spikes — tournament format proven for retention
Social features gap: Inspiror has Web Share API (export) but no in-app gallery where kids browse each other's projects
Actionable: 'Hall of Fame' read-only gallery page (no auth required) where exported HTML projects are submitted and displayed by title/screenshot
Risk: social features require moderation pipeline (COPPA compliance) — defer until content moderation is implemented (currently deferred to 2026-03-18)

queries.query: AI voice interaction kids apps text-to-speech speech-to-text accessibility 2026
keyFindings: Child TTS market growing strongly: specialized child AI voices (excited, gentle humor, surprise) available via Speechgen, Resemble AI, Narakeet, Acapela Group
Web Speech API (SpeechRecognition + SpeechSynthesis) is built into all modern browsers — zero dependency cost for Inspiror
Voice-first coding lowers barrier for early readers (ages 8-9) and children with dyslexia — strong accessibility and COPPA alignment story
Opportunity: 'Voice mode' for chat input — child speaks to Builder Buddy instead of typing; Web Speech API sends transcript to existing LLM pipeline
Opportunity: Buddy speaks responses via SpeechSynthesis using a child-appropriate voice — transforms text chat into a more natural tutoring experience
Inspiror already has useVoice.ts hook — this is partially scaffolded; extending it to cover both STT input and TTS output is a low-effort, high-impact feature
Therapeutic angle: children with speech delays or anxiety benefit from AI voice companions — broadens addressable market beyond coding-focused kids
